question_text: A power station produces 800 MW of electrical power but consumes 2000 MW of energy from burning fuel. A student claims the power station 'destroys' 1200 MW of energy. Explain why this statement is incorrect and calculate the actual efficiency of the power station.
correct_answer: The statement is incorrect because energy cannot be destroyed; it is only converted from one form to another. The 1200 MW is not destroyed but converted into heat energy released to the environment. The efficiency of the power station is 40% (calculated as 800 MW ÷ 2000 MW × 100%).
explanation: This question tests two important ideas: (1) Energy conservation - energy is never destroyed, only converted between different forms. The 'lost' 1200 MW becomes heat energy that escapes to the environment rather than being useful electrical energy. (2) Efficiency calculations - efficiency = useful energy output ÷ total energy input × 100%. Here, only 800 MW out of 2000 MW becomes electricity, so 40% of the fuel energy is converted usefully. The remaining 60% becomes wasted heat.
